# Engineering Glossary

A searchable central reference for the Developer Ecosystem learning track. Use browser search (`Ctrl/Cmd + F`) to find terms. Each entry uses the same learning-first structure.

## Git

- **Definition:** Git is a modern software engineering term used to describe a tool, practice, system component, or operational concern in professional software delivery.
- **Beginner Explanation:** In simple words, it helps teams build, connect, ship, secure, measure, or maintain software more reliably.
- **Practical Meaning:** When you see `Git` in documentation or a job description, ask what problem it solves, what data it touches, who operates it, and what happens when it fails.
- **Industry Usage:** Engineers use this term in design discussions, code reviews, deployment plans, incident reports, architecture diagrams, and onboarding notes.
- **Related Concepts:** GitLab, Bitbucket, Repositories.
- **Common Mistakes:** Memorizing the name without understanding the workflow, assuming every project needs it, or ignoring cost, security, and maintenance trade-offs.
- **Cross References:** Start with [Version Control](./modules/01-version-control-collaboration.md), [CI/CD](./modules/06-ci-cd.md), [Security](./modules/21-security.md), and [Production Readiness](./modules/33-production-readiness.md).

## Dependency Trees

- **Definition:** Dependency Trees is a modern software engineering term used to describe a tool, practice, system component, or operational concern in professional software delivery.
- **Beginner Explanation:** In simple words, it helps teams build, connect, ship, secure, measure, or maintain software more reliably.
- **Practical Meaning:** When you see `Dependency Trees` in documentation or a job description, ask what problem it solves, what data it touches, who operates it, and what happens when it fails.
- **Industry Usage:** Engineers use this term in design discussions, code reviews, deployment plans, incident reports, architecture diagrams, and onboarding notes.
- **Related Concepts:** Vercel, Netlify, Cloudflare Pages.
- **Common Mistakes:** Memorizing the name without understanding the workflow, assuming every project needs it, or ignoring cost, security, and maintenance trade-offs.
- **Cross References:** Start with [Version Control](./modules/01-version-control-collaboration.md), [CI/CD](./modules/06-ci-cd.md), [Security](./modules/21-security.md), and [Production Readiness](./modules/33-production-readiness.md).

- **Beginner Explanation:** In simple words, it helps teams build, connect, ship, secure, measure, or maintain software more reliably.
- **Practical Meaning:** When you see `RBAC` in documentation or a job description, ask what problem it solves, what data it touches, who operates it, and what happens when it fails.
- **Industry Usage:** Engineers use this term in design discussions, code reviews, deployment plans, incident reports, architecture diagrams, and onboarding notes.
- **Related Concepts:** REST, GraphQL, RPC.
- **Common Mistakes:** Memorizing the name without understanding the workflow, assuming every project needs it, or ignoring cost, security, and maintenance trade-offs.
- **Cross References:** Start with [Version Control](./modules/01-version-control-collaboration.md), [CI/CD](./modules/06-ci-cd.md), [Security](./modules/21-security.md), and [Production Readiness](./modules/33-production-readiness.md).

- **Beginner Explanation:** In simple words, it helps teams build, connect, ship, secure, measure, or maintain software more reliably.
- **Practical Meaning:** When you see `UDP` in documentation or a job description, ask what problem it solves, what data it touches, who operates it, and what happens when it fails.
- **Industry Usage:** Engineers use this term in design discussions, code reviews, deployment plans, incident reports, architecture diagrams, and onboarding notes.
- **Related Concepts:** HTTP/2, HTTP/3, QUIC.
- **Common Mistakes:** Memorizing the name without understanding the workflow, assuming every project needs it, or ignoring cost, security, and maintenance trade-offs.
- **Cross References:** Start with [Version Control](./modules/01-version-control-collaboration.md), [CI/CD](./modules/06-ci-cd.md), [Security](./modules/21-security.md), and [Production Readiness](./modules/33-production-readiness.md).

- **Beginner Explanation:** In simple words, it helps teams build, connect, ship, secure, measure, or maintain software more reliably.
- **Practical Meaning:** When you see `Architecture Limit` in documentation or a job description, ask what problem it solves, what data it touches, who operates it, and what happens when it fails.
- **Industry Usage:** Engineers use this term in design discussions, code reviews, deployment plans, incident reports, architecture diagrams, and onboarding notes.
- **Related Concepts:** Architecture Flag, Architecture Session, Architecture Event.
- **Common Mistakes:** Memorizing the name without understanding the workflow, assuming every project needs it, or ignoring cost, security, and maintenance trade-offs.
- **Cross References:** Start with [Version Control](./modules/01-version-control-collaboration.md), [CI/CD](./modules/06-ci-cd.md), [Security](./modules/21-security.md), and [Production Readiness](./modules/33-production-readiness.md).

## DevOps Pattern

- **Definition:** DevOps Pattern is a modern software engineering term used to describe a tool, practice, system component, or operational concern in professional software delivery.
- **Beginner Explanation:** In simple words, it helps teams build, connect, ship, secure, measure, or maintain software more reliably.
- **Practical Meaning:** When you see `DevOps Pattern` in documentation or a job description, ask what problem it solves, what data it touches, who operates it, and what happens when it fails.
- **Industry Usage:** Engineers use this term in design discussions, code reviews, deployment plans, incident reports, architecture diagrams, and onboarding notes.
- **Related Concepts:** DevOps Metric, DevOps Pipeline, DevOps Gateway.
- **Common Mistakes:** Memorizing the name without understanding the workflow, assuming every project needs it, or ignoring cost, security, and maintenance trade-offs.
- **Cross References:** Start with [Version Control](./modules/01-version-control-collaboration.md), [CI/CD](./modules/06-ci-cd.md), [Security](./modules/21-security.md), and [Production Readiness](./modules/33-production-readiness.md).

- **Beginner Explanation:** In simple words, it helps teams build, connect, ship, secure, measure, or maintain software more reliably.
- **Practical Meaning:** When you see `Testing Budget` in documentation or a job description, ask what problem it solves, what data it touches, who operates it, and what happens when it fails.
- **Industry Usage:** Engineers use this term in design discussions, code reviews, deployment plans, incident reports, architecture diagrams, and onboarding notes.
- **Related Concepts:** Testing Version, Testing Flag, Testing Session.
- **Common Mistakes:** Memorizing the name without understanding the workflow, assuming every project needs it, or ignoring cost, security, and maintenance trade-offs.
- **Cross References:** Start with [Version Control](./modules/01-version-control-collaboration.md), [CI/CD](./modules/06-ci-cd.md), [Security](./modules/21-security.md), and [Production Readiness](./modules/33-production-readiness.md).
